subject: economics
description: IB DP Economics covers Microeconomics, Macroeconomics, Global Economy (international trade, development). 9 key concepts: scarcity, choice, efficiency, equity, etc.

nodes.name: 稀缺与选择
curriculum_points: Students will be able to explain the central economic problem of scarcity, choice, and opportunity cost, using production possibility curve (PPC) models to illustrate these concepts.
Students will be able to evaluate the trade-offs and opportunity costs involved in economic decision-making by individuals, firms, and governments.

nodes.name: 供求分析
curriculum_points: Students will be able to construct and analyse demand and supply diagrams to illustrate market equilibrium, and explain the causes and consequences of shifts in and movements along these curves.
Students will be able to apply the model of demand and supply to real-world markets, explaining how the price mechanism allocates resources and rations goods.

nodes.name: 弹性
curriculum_points: Students will be able to calculate and interpret price elasticity of demand (PED), price elasticity of supply (PES), income elasticity of demand (YED), and cross-price elasticity of demand (XED).
Students will be able to evaluate the significance of elasticity for stakeholders, including consumers, producers, and governments, in areas such as tax incidence and revenue.

nodes.name: 市场失灵
curriculum_points: Students will be able to explain, with examples, the concepts of negative externalities, positive externalities, common pool resources, and public goods as sources of market failure.
Students will be able to illustrate market failure using marginal social benefit (MSB) and marginal social cost (MSC) diagrams, identifying the welfare loss.

nodes.name: 政府干预
curriculum_points: Students will be able to evaluate the use of government intervention methods, including indirect taxes, subsidies, tradable permits, and direct provision, to correct market failures.
Students will be able to analyse the consequences of government intervention, including price ceilings and price floors, on market outcomes, stakeholders, and government budgets.

nodes.name: 厂商理论（HL）
curriculum_points: HL students will be able to calculate and interpret revenue, cost, and profit data for firms in different market structures (perfect competition, monopoly, monopolistic competition).
HL students will be able to diagrammatically analyse short-run and long-run equilibrium positions for profit-maximizing firms and evaluate the efficiency outcomes of different market structures.

nodes.name: GDP 与国民收入
curriculum_points: Students will be able to explain the circular flow of income model and calculate nominal and real GDP using the expenditure, income, and output methods.
Students will be able to evaluate the uses and limitations of GDP statistics as a measure of economic well-being and standard of living.

nodes.name: AD-AS 模型
curriculum_points: Students will be able to construct and interpret the aggregate demand (AD) and aggregate supply (AS) model to illustrate macroeconomic equilibrium, the business cycle, and inflationary/deflationary gaps.
Students will be able to analyse the causes of shifts in the AD and AS curves and evaluate their impacts on the price level, real output, and employment.

nodes.name: 失业
curriculum_points: Students will be able to distinguish between the main types of unemployment (frictional, structural, cyclical, seasonal) and explain their causes.
Students will be able to calculate the unemployment rate and evaluate the economic and social consequences of unemployment for different stakeholders.

nodes.name: 通货膨胀
curriculum_points: Students will be able to distinguish between demand-pull and cost-push inflation using AD/AS analysis and calculate inflation rates from a set of price index data.
Students will be able to evaluate the consequences of inflation and deflation for consumers, firms, the government, and the economy as a whole.

nodes.name: 财政与货币政策
curriculum_points: Students will be able to explain how expansionary and contractionary fiscal policy (changes in government spending and taxation) and monetary policy (changes in interest rates) can be used to influence aggregate demand.
Students will be able to evaluate the effectiveness, strengths, and limitations of demand-side policies in achieving macroeconomic objectives such as low unemployment and price stability.

nodes.name: 经济增长
curriculum_points: Students will be able to distinguish between economic growth (an increase in real GDP) and economic development (broader improvements in living standards).

Students will be able to analyse the sources of long-term economic growth (increases in quantity/quality of factors of production, technological advances) and evaluate its potential consequences, including environmental impacts.

domains.name: 全球经济 / Global Economy / Global Economy

nodes.name: 国际贸易
curriculum_points: Students will be able to explain the theory of comparative advantage and illustrate the potential gains from trade and specialization using diagrams.
Students will be able to analyse the arguments for and against trade protectionism (tariffs, quotas, subsidies) and evaluate their impacts on stakeholders.

nodes.name: 汇率
curriculum_points: Students will be able to distinguish between floating, fixed, and managed exchange rate systems and illustrate how they are determined in a foreign exchange market diagram.
Students will be able to analyse the causes and consequences of exchange rate fluctuations for a nation's inflation, unemployment, current account balance, and economic growth.

nodes.name: 国际收支
curriculum_points: Students will be able to explain the structure of the balance of payments accounts, distinguishing between the current account and the capital/financial account.
Students will be able to analyse the causes and consequences of current account deficits and surpluses, and evaluate policy responses to address imbalances.

nodes.name: 经济一体化
curriculum_points: Students will be able to explain the different forms of economic integration, from preferential trade agreements to monetary unions, using real-world examples.
Students will be able to evaluate the potential economic benefits (e.g., trade creation) and costs (e.g., trade diversion, loss of sovereignty) of regional economic integration.

nodes.name: 发展经济学
curriculum_points: Students will be able to compare and contrast alternative measures of economic development, including the Human Development Index (HDI) and the Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI).
Students will be able to evaluate the role of domestic factors (e.g., education, institutional quality) and international factors (e.g., debt, aid, trade) in economic development.

domains.name: 关键概念 / Key Concepts / Key Concepts

nodes.name: 效率与公平
curriculum_points: Students will be able to distinguish between allocative, productive, and dynamic efficiency and explain the trade-off between equity and efficiency in economic policy.
Students will be able to evaluate government policies, such as progressive taxation and transfer payments, aimed at promoting greater equity (fairness) in the distribution of income and wealth.

nodes.name: 可持续性
curriculum_points: Students will be able to explain the concept of sustainable development and evaluate the role of market-based (e.g., carbon taxes) and government-led strategies in promoting environmental sustainability.
Students will be able to critically assess the potential conflict between the macroeconomic objectives of economic growth and environmental sustainability.

nodes.name: 不平等
curriculum_points: Students will be able to analyse the causes of income and wealth inequality within and between countries using tools such as the Lorenz curve and Gini coefficient.
Students will be able to evaluate the economic and social impacts of inequality and assess the effectiveness of policies designed to reduce it.
